pageMeta.title: AI Agents for Interior Design Client Workflows
pageMeta.description: DocsBot helps interior design firms answer client questions, qualify projects, and surface approved specs from your own design knowledge.

hero.eyebrow: AI support and knowledge agents for design studios
hero.headline: DocsBot AI agents keep interior design clients and teams aligned
hero.subheadline: Train DocsBot on your portfolio, service guides, FAQs, product notes, vendor documents, proposal language, care instructions, and internal SOPs so clients get fast, source-grounded answers while your team protects time for design work.

proofBullets.text: Answer service, process, materials, timeline, pricing-policy, and care questions from approved studio content.
Collect project scope, style preferences, budget range, location, timeline, and contact details before a consultation.
Retrieve internal specs, vendor notes, installation guidance, handoff details, and brand standards for your team.
Route complex design, procurement, budget, or contract questions to the right human with a clear conversation summary.

businessClarity.whatDocsBotDoes: DocsBot helps interior design firms create AI agents trained on their own knowledge. A customer-facing agent can answer prospect and client questions through a website widget, while a private internal agent helps designers, project coordinators, procurement teams, and sales staff find the right answer from studio-approved sources.

whoItHelps.roleContext: Keep inquiries moving without turning every first-touch question into a manual email thread. DocsBot can explain your process, collect project-fit details, and prepare a handoff for a human designer when the prospect is ready.

bullets.text: Qualify leads by project type, timeline, location, budget range, room count, and preferred follow-up channel.
Answer repeat questions about consultation fees, deliverables, purchasing policies, communication cadence, and next steps.
Surface unanswered topics in analytics so your team can improve FAQs, service pages, and onboarding documents.

whoItHelps.roleContext: Give the team one place to ask about approved vendor notes, client-facing wording, project procedures, installation guidance, and handoff details without digging through folders or past messages.

bullets.text: Retrieve studio SOPs, product-care notes, finish rules, procurement steps, and client communication templates.
Summarize project intake conversations so designers start with clearer context before the first paid consultation.
Escalate sensitive budget, contract, or design-judgment decisions to humans while keeping routine answers consistent.

whoItHelps.roleContext: Respond faster to prospective clients, homeowners, developers, and real estate partners while keeping answers aligned with your studio’s service scope and brand voice.

bullets.text: Interior designers are projected to have about 7,800 U.S. openings each year from 2024 to 2034, making repeatable knowledge access useful as teams hire, replace, or train staff.
Send qualified inquiry summaries to customer-configured CRM, helpdesk, forms, inbox, Slack, Microsoft Teams, or other approved workflows.
Support multilingual client questions when your studio serves relocation buyers, international homeowners, or hospitality clients.

evidenceSources.claim: The U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics projects about 7,800 openings for interior designers each year on average from 2024 to 2034.
evidenceSources.sourceTitle: U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics Occupational Outlook Handbook: Interior Designers

evidenceSources.metric: 47% of interior designers expected improved product and material availability
evidenceSources.claim: Houzz reported that interior designers were the most optimistic group about product and material availability improving in 2024, at 47%.
evidenceSources.sourceTitle: 2024 U.S. Houzz State of the Industry

evidenceSources.metric: $235 billion projected U.S. kitchen and bath spending in 2025
evidenceSources.claim: NKBA reported projected U.S. residential kitchen and bath spending of $235 billion in 2025, up from $233 billion the prior year.
evidenceSources.sourceTitle: Key Demographic Trends Drive Expected K&B Remodeling Growth

evidenceSources.metric: Interior design profession growth over the last decade
evidenceSources.claim: ASID’s 2024 outlook describes a decade of growth and increasing diversity in the interior design community.
evidenceSources.sourceTitle: 2024 ASID State of Interior Design Outlook Report

agentUseCases.name: Project-fit intake agent
agentUseCases.problem: Prospects often ask broad questions about pricing, availability, process, and whether the studio handles their type of project before they are ready for a consultation.
agentUseCases.agentWorkflow: DocsBot answers from approved service pages, FAQs, proposal language, portfolio descriptions, and consultation policies. It collects scope, property type, rooms, style preferences, timeline, budget range, location, and contact details, then prepares a concise handoff for the design or sales team.
agentUseCases.liveContextOrTools: With approved AI Actions, Skills, APIs, connectors, or workflow automations, the agent can submit a lead form, create a task, notify the team, or request a meeting through a configured scheduling tool.
exampleQuestionsOrRequests: Do you take full-home furnishing projects in my area?
What should I prepare before booking a design consultation?
Can you help with a kitchen refresh if I already have a contractor?
agentUseCases.businessOutcome: Fewer unqualified back-and-forth emails and clearer first consultations because the team receives structured context before responding.

agentUseCases.name: Material and product knowledge assistant
agentUseCases.problem: Clients and team members ask repeat questions about finishes, fabrics, product care, lead times, vendor policies, substitutions, and installation constraints.
agentUseCases.agentWorkflow: DocsBot retrieves answers from vendor PDFs, product notes, internal specification documents, care guides, and approved client-facing explanations. If the question affects pricing, warranties, availability, or final selection, it can route the request to a human with the relevant source context.
agentUseCases.liveContextOrTools: When connected to approved live systems, the agent can look up current status, summarize a vendor note, draft a client update, or create a follow-up task. Interior designers were the most optimistic Houzz group about improved product and material availability in 2024, at 47%, but availability still requires clear communication when choices change.
exampleQuestionsOrRequests: What is the care guidance for this performance fabric?
Which countertop options did we approve for the lake house project?
Can you summarize the vendor’s installation restrictions for this fixture?
agentUseCases.businessOutcome: More consistent product answers and faster internal lookup without relying on one coordinator to remember every vendor detail.

agentUseCases.name: Client onboarding and project-status explainer
agentUseCases.problem: New clients may not understand deliverables, revision limits, purchasing steps, procurement timelines, approvals, or how decisions move from concept to installation.
agentUseCases.agentWorkflow: DocsBot answers onboarding questions from welcome packets, contract summaries, project guides, milestone documents, and communication policies. It can explain what happens next, what the client needs to provide, and when a human should step in for a decision or exception.
agentUseCases.liveContextOrTools: With customer-configured Skills or APIs, the agent can retrieve approved project metadata, create a task for missing information, or route a client question to the project manager. Sensitive budget, contract, and change-order decisions should remain human-reviewed.
exampleQuestionsOrRequests: What happens after the concept presentation?
Where do I upload inspiration images and measurements?
Who approves furniture purchases before ordering?
agentUseCases.businessOutcome: Clients feel guided through the process while project managers spend less time repeating the same procedural explanations.

agentUseCases.name: Portfolio and style discovery agent
agentUseCases.problem: Prospects browse past work but still need help connecting services, design style, project type, budget expectations, and next steps.
agentUseCases.agentWorkflow: DocsBot uses your portfolio copy, case studies, room descriptions, service pages, and FAQs to explain project examples and guide visitors toward the right inquiry path. It can ask clarifying questions and collect inspiration preferences without pretending to replace the designer’s creative judgment.
agentUseCases.liveContextOrTools: The agent can use structured intake forms and customer-configured routing rules to separate residential, commercial, staging, furnishing, renovation, and consulting requests.
exampleQuestionsOrRequests: Which of your projects is closest to a warm minimalist family room?
Do you handle furnishings only, or do you work with contractors too?
Can you explain the difference between your design consultation and full-service package?
agentUseCases.businessOutcome: Better self-education before the call and more informed inquiries from people who understand the studio’s scope.

agentUseCases.name: Internal SOP and brand-voice agent
agentUseCases.problem: As studios grow, team members need quick access to standards for client emails, proposal language, procurement, install days, vendor communication, and brand voice.
agentUseCases.agentWorkflow: DocsBot trains on internal SOPs, templates, onboarding guides, policies, wikis, PDFs, and approved examples. Staff can ask practical questions and get source-grounded answers that reflect how the studio actually works.
agentUseCases.liveContextOrTools: Private access can be delivered through internal chat experiences, Slack, Microsoft Teams, or API-based workflows when configured. MCP Connectors can also connect agents to remote Model Context Protocol servers that expose tools your team already uses.
exampleQuestionsOrRequests: What is our client update format after a site visit?
How do we document a backorder and alternative recommendation?
Where is the checklist for installation day?
agentUseCases.businessOutcome: Faster onboarding, fewer process inconsistencies, and less time spent searching shared drives or asking senior staff repeat questions.

agentUseCases.name: Kitchen and bath inquiry triage
agentUseCases.problem: Kitchen and bath projects involve high homeowner investment, many material decisions, and frequent questions about scope, timelines, and professional fit.
agentUseCases.agentWorkflow: DocsBot answers from kitchen and bath service pages, remodeling FAQs, design packages, qualification rules, and project planning guides. It can collect project details, flag whether the prospect needs a designer, contractor, or both, and route the inquiry to the right team member.
agentUseCases.liveContextOrTools: With approved workflow actions, the agent can create an intake record, notify a specialist, request photos or measurements, and send a scheduling handoff. NKBA reported projected U.S. residential kitchen and bath spending of $235 billion in 2025.
exampleQuestionsOrRequests: Can you help us select finishes if our contractor is already hired?
What information do you need before a kitchen design consultation?
Do you work on primary bathroom remodels with aging-in-place features?
agentUseCases.businessOutcome: Higher-quality remodeling conversations and fewer mismatched inquiries for teams that specialize in kitchens, baths, or renovation design.

customerFacingAgent.headline: A client-facing AI agent that sounds like your studio
customerFacingAgent.intro: Place DocsBot on your website or client portal to answer routine questions from your approved knowledge. It can help prospects understand your services, help active clients find process answers, and escalate nuanced design or commercial decisions to your team.

requests.request: “What does your full-service interior design process include?”
requests.agentResponseStrategy: Answer from service pages, consultation guides, FAQs, and onboarding documents, then ask whether the visitor wants to share project scope and timeline.
requests.optionalAction: Collect project-fit details and forward a structured inquiry through a customer-configured lead workflow.

requests.request: “Can you help me choose between two material options?”
requests.agentResponseStrategy: Provide source-grounded care, durability, style, or maintenance context from approved material notes, then make clear that final design recommendations are handled by the studio.
requests.optionalAction: Create a follow-up task or route the question to the assigned designer with the conversation summary.

requests.request: “How much does a project like this usually cost?”
requests.agentResponseStrategy: Explain published pricing models, consultation fees, budget ranges, or qualification guidance only from approved studio content, while routing custom estimates to a human.
requests.optionalAction: Collect budget range, room count, property type, and timeline before handing the inquiry to the right person.

requests.request: “What is the status of my selections or order?”
requests.agentResponseStrategy: If connected to approved live context, look up permitted status information or explain the standard procurement process from onboarding materials.
requests.optionalAction: Trigger a notification, create a ticket, or prepare a human handoff when status is missing or requires review.

requests.request: “Do you work with clients who speak another language?”
requests.agentResponseStrategy: Use DocsBot’s multilingual answer capabilities to respond from the same approved studio sources while preserving consistent policy and service information.
requests.optionalAction: Route the conversation to a bilingual team member or preferred follow-up channel when configured.

requests.request: “Can I see examples similar to my home?”
requests.agentResponseStrategy: Point visitors to relevant portfolio examples, room types, project descriptions, and service fit content without inventing projects or visual claims.
requests.optionalAction: Ask for style preferences and project type, then attach the summary to a consultation request.

internalAgent.headline: A private knowledge agent for design, procurement, and operations
internalAgent.intro: DocsBot can serve as an internal assistant trained on the documents your team already relies on: SOPs, vendor notes, templates, policies, project playbooks, brand standards, and onboarding material.

workflows.name: Procurement answer desk
workflows.problem: Teams lose time hunting for vendor rules, order procedures, installation notes, and client-facing explanations for delays or substitutions.
workflows.agentWorkflow: The internal agent retrieves approved procurement SOPs, vendor PDFs, order checklists, and care guidance. It can summarize the answer and cite the underlying source so staff know where the instruction came from.
workflows.optionalAction: Create a task, draft a client update, or route a flagged issue to procurement through customer-configured workflows.
workflows.outcome: Faster and more consistent answers for materials, orders, care guidance, and follow-ups.

workflows.name: New-hire studio onboarding
workflows.problem: New designers and coordinators need to learn how the studio communicates, documents selections, handles revisions, and prepares client deliverables.
workflows.agentWorkflow: DocsBot answers onboarding questions from training materials, templates, SOPs, internal wikis, and approved examples. Analytics can reveal which topics confuse new hires most often.
workflows.optionalAction: Send repeated unanswered questions to the operations lead for documentation updates.
workflows.outcome: Reduced dependency on senior staff for repeat training and a more consistent client experience.

workflows.name: Proposal and sales enablement support
workflows.problem: Sales or studio leads need quick access to service definitions, proposal language, objection handling, project examples, and qualification rules before responding to a prospect.
workflows.agentWorkflow: The agent retrieves approved proposal wording, service package details, portfolio context, FAQs, and pricing-policy language. It can draft a response for human review without making commitments outside approved content.
workflows.optionalAction: Draft a follow-up email, create a CRM task, or summarize account context when the customer has configured approved Skills, APIs, or connectors.
workflows.outcome: More consistent presales responses while keeping humans responsible for custom pricing, contracts, and final scope.

workflows.name: Project handoff summaries
workflows.problem: Important details can be scattered across intake forms, emails, meeting notes, client preferences, and internal documents.
workflows.agentWorkflow: DocsBot can summarize configured sources and conversations into a structured handoff: goals, rooms, constraints, budget signals, timeline, open questions, and next actions.
workflows.optionalAction: Post the summary to an approved team channel, create a task, or attach it to a project record through customer-configured automation.
workflows.outcome: Cleaner handoffs between sales, design, procurement, and project management.

docsbotFeatureMapping.feature: Source-grounded answers from your own design knowledge
docsbotFeatureMapping.mainLandingPageContext: DocsBot trains AI agents on configured company sources such as websites, documents, help content, PDFs, internal files, and knowledge bases.
docsbotFeatureMapping.industryApplication: Use approved service pages, portfolio copy, material notes, FAQs, vendor PDFs, proposal language, and onboarding guides so clients and staff receive answers tied to your studio’s actual process.

docsbotFeatureMapping.feature: Website widget and private team access
docsbotFeatureMapping.mainLandingPageContext: DocsBot agents can appear in customer-facing chat experiences and private internal team workflows, including Slack, Microsoft Teams, API, and configured helpdesk-style flows.
docsbotFeatureMapping.industryApplication: Place a client-facing agent on your studio website for inquiries, and give staff a private knowledge agent for SOPs, procurement questions, brand voice, and project procedures.

docsbotFeatureMapping.feature: AI Actions, Skills, APIs, connectors, and workflow automation
docsbotFeatureMapping.mainLandingPageContext: DocsBot AI Actions and Skills let agents take bounded actions such as lookups, summaries, routing, record creation, updates, notifications, and workflow triggers when configured by the customer.
docsbotFeatureMapping.industryApplication: Collect lead details, create a consultation task, request missing photos, summarize intake, route an order-status question, or notify the right team member through approved studio workflows.

docsbotFeatureMapping.mainLandingPageContext: DocsBot provides analytics, logs, reports, topic management, conversation review, satisfaction and resolution analysis, and knowledge-gap discovery.
docsbotFeatureMapping.industryApplication: See which design-process, pricing, deliverable, material, and timeline questions keep repeating so your studio can improve FAQs, onboarding guides, and service pages.

docsbotFeatureMapping.mainLandingPageContext: DocsBot offers SOC 2 Type II certification, GDPR-ready governance, encryption in transit and at rest, granular access controls, role-based permissions, and does not use customer data to train public models.
docsbotFeatureMapping.industryApplication: Protect private client, vendor, pricing, and project knowledge while giving the right team members access to the sources they need.

docsbotFeatureMapping.mainLandingPageContext: DocsBot can escalate when a question is complex, sensitive, or needs a person.
docsbotFeatureMapping.industryApplication: Let the agent handle routine answers while routing custom design recommendations, contract issues, budget exceptions, procurement disputes, and final approvals to your team.

trustClaims: Train agents on your own content library so answers stay aligned with your studio knowledge.
Support customer and stakeholder communication with consistent, source-grounded responses.
Use DocsBot for both client-facing support and private internal knowledge workflows.
trustAndProof.whyTheseReviewsFit: Interior design firms win trust through clear communication, consistent process, and fast access to the right details. DocsBot proof across real estate communication, owned-content training, customer support, and internal decision workflows shows how teams can reduce repetitive questions without losing control of the client experience.

faq.question: Can DocsBot qualify interior design inquiries before they reach our principal designer?
faq.answer: Yes. DocsBot can ask structured intake questions about project type, rooms, location, timeline, budget range, style preferences, contractor status, and preferred follow-up channel. It can then summarize the conversation for your team so a human can decide whether the inquiry fits your service model and availability.

faq.question: Will the agent give design advice that conflicts with our creative direction?
faq.answer: DocsBot should be trained to answer from approved studio content and to avoid pretending it is the designer. It can explain your process, portfolio, material notes, care guidance, and service options, while routing subjective recommendations, final selections, budget exceptions, and contract decisions to a human.

faq.question: Can we use it for both website leads and internal studio questions?
faq.answer: Yes. A customer-facing DocsBot agent can live on your website to answer prospect and client questions, while a private internal agent can help staff find SOPs, vendor notes, procurement steps, proposal language, install-day checklists, and brand-voice guidance from approved sources.

faq.question: How does DocsBot help if most of our knowledge is scattered across PDFs, Google Drive, proposals, care guides, and old onboarding docs?
faq.answer: DocsBot is designed for source-grounded answers from your configured knowledge sources. Interior design firms can train agents on service pages, portfolio descriptions, vendor PDFs, product-care documents, proposal templates, client welcome packets, SOPs, and internal files so staff and clients get answers tied to studio-approved material.

faq.question: Can the agent explain our pricing without creating unrealistic expectations?
faq.answer: Yes, if your source content defines what can be shared. DocsBot can explain published consultation fees, package descriptions, billing policies, budget ranges, or qualification guidance from approved content, then escalate custom estimates, scope changes, discounts, or contract questions to your team.

faq.question: Can DocsBot reduce back-and-forth during first consultations?
faq.answer: It can help by collecting context before the call: property type, rooms, goals, existing contractor involvement, decision makers, budget signals, timeline, inspiration preferences, and open questions. The handoff gives designers a cleaner starting point instead of spending the first meeting reconstructing basic details.

faq.question: What if a client asks about order status, backorders, substitutions, or procurement timing?
faq.answer: DocsBot can answer standard procurement-process questions from onboarding guides and SOPs. If you configure approved live context through Skills, APIs, connectors, or workflows, it can look up permitted status information, create a task, notify the right person, or prepare a human handoff when the answer needs review.

faq.question: Can it help our team keep material and product answers consistent?
faq.answer: Yes. DocsBot can retrieve approved care instructions, vendor notes, specification documents, installation constraints, finish rules, and substitution guidance. That is useful because product and material availability can still require careful communication; Houzz reported that 47% of interior designers expected improved product and material availability in 2024.

faq.question: How do we prevent the agent from inventing vendor policies or product specs?
faq.answer: Train it on approved sources and set instructions to answer only from those materials when accuracy matters. For missing, outdated, or conflicting vendor information, the agent should say it does not have enough approved context and route the question to procurement or the assigned designer with the conversation summary.

faq.question: Can DocsBot support kitchen and bath remodeling inquiries separately from furnishing-only leads?
faq.answer: Yes. You can define intake rules that ask whether the prospect needs design, contractor coordination, finish selection, cabinetry guidance, or furnishings only. That matters for a large category: NKBA reported projected U.S. residential kitchen and bath spending of $235 billion in 2025.

faq.question: Will clients feel like we are replacing the personal studio experience with automation?
faq.answer: The best fit is not replacing designers; it is giving clients faster answers to routine questions and clearer paths to a human. DocsBot can reflect your brand voice, explain your process, collect context, and escalate nuanced design, budget, procurement, or relationship-sensitive issues to the right person.

faq.question: Can DocsBot work with our CRM, forms, scheduling tools, Slack, Microsoft Teams, or project workflows?
faq.answer: DocsBot can support customer-configured workflows through AI Actions, Skills, APIs, connectors, forms, webhooks, and approved automations. That can include forwarding qualified leads, creating tasks, notifying a team channel, requesting a meeting, or attaching intake summaries, without claiming a one-size-fits-all native integration.

faq.question: What information should we have ready before implementing an interior design AI agent?
faq.answer: Start with your service pages, FAQ, consultation policy, pricing-policy language, proposal templates, client onboarding guides, portfolio descriptions, procurement SOPs, product-care notes, vendor PDFs, and escalation rules. The cleaner your approved source content is, the more useful and consistent the agent will be.

faq.question: How long will our team need to maintain the knowledge base?
faq.answer: Plan for light ongoing ownership. Someone should update pricing policies, service scope, vendor notes, lead-time language, care instructions, and onboarding documents as they change. DocsBot analytics and conversation review can show repeated unanswered questions, helping your operations lead decide which documents need improvement.

faq.question: Can DocsBot help onboard new designers, procurement coordinators, or client experience staff?
faq.answer: Yes. A private internal agent can answer questions from SOPs, templates, onboarding guides, brand standards, and internal wikis. That is relevant for firms that hire or replace staff over time; BLS projects about 7,800 U.S. interior designer openings each year on average from 2024 to 2034.

faq.question: How do we measure whether this is paying off?
faq.answer: Track fewer repetitive inquiry emails, faster lead response, higher-quality consultation handoffs, reduced time searching for vendor or SOP details, fewer repeated client-process questions, and improved content based on analytics. For internal use, compare how often staff find answers without interrupting a principal or project manager.

faq.question: Can we see what clients and staff are asking the agent?
faq.answer: Yes. DocsBot provides analytics, logs, reports, topic management, conversation review, satisfaction and resolution analysis, and knowledge-gap discovery. For a design studio, those insights can reveal confusing service pages, repeated pricing objections, unclear procurement steps, missing care guidance, or training gaps.

faq.question: What happens when a prospect is not a fit for our studio?
faq.answer: DocsBot can explain your published service scope and collect enough information to identify likely mismatches based on your rules, such as location, project size, timeline, budget range, or service type. It can then route the inquiry, suggest the right next step from approved content, or prepare a polite human-reviewed response.

faq.question: Can the agent answer in our brand voice rather than sounding generic?
faq.answer: Yes. DocsBot can be trained on your website copy, portfolio descriptions, proposal language, email templates, FAQs, and brand standards so responses match your approved tone. For sensitive situations, it can draft language for human review instead of sending a final commitment automatically.

faq.question: How does DocsBot handle client privacy, vendor pricing, and internal project information?
faq.answer: DocsBot offers SOC 2 Type II certification, GDPR-ready governance, encryption in transit and at rest, granular access controls, role-based permissions, and does not use customer data to train public models. You can separate client-facing knowledge from private internal sources so visitors do not see staff-only information.

faq.question: Can we restrict who can ask about internal SOPs, vendor terms, or pricing guidance?
faq.answer: Yes. DocsBot supports granular access controls and role-based permissions. A studio can make general service information available to website visitors while limiting vendor notes, procurement SOPs, proposal language, internal pricing guidance, and project documents to authorized team members.

faq.question: What if our design firm works with international homeowners or relocation clients?
faq.answer: DocsBot can answer in more than 100 languages while still drawing from the same approved studio sources. That can help prospects understand your services, onboarding steps, and communication policies, while routing nuanced cultural, contractual, or project-specific questions to the right human.

faq.question: How is this different from adding a basic chatbot to our website?
faq.answer: DocsBot is better positioned as an AI agent trained on your own knowledge, not a generic scripted chatbot. It can provide source-grounded answers, summarize intake, surface knowledge gaps, support internal team questions, and take bounded actions through customer-configured Skills, APIs, connectors, or workflows.

faq.question: Can DocsBot help with commercial, hospitality, or developer clients as well as residential work?
faq.answer: Yes, if your approved content covers those workflows. You can train separate or segmented knowledge around residential design, commercial interiors, hospitality packages, model units, developer partnerships, procurement rules, and handoff requirements so the agent routes each inquiry appropriately.

faq.question: Will we still control final proposals, contracts, budgets, and purchasing decisions?
faq.answer: Yes. DocsBot can explain approved policies, collect context, draft summaries, and route questions, but your team should remain responsible for final scope, pricing, contracts, procurement commitments, substitutions, and creative recommendations. Escalation rules help keep high-impact decisions human-reviewed.

faq.question: Can DocsBot support a growing studio without forcing us into a heavy enterprise implementation?
faq.answer: Yes. Many firms can start with a focused website intake agent or a private SOP assistant, then expand into procurement lookups, onboarding support, client-status explanations, and workflow actions as their content and processes mature. The practical path is to automate repeatable questions before sensitive decisions.

faq.question: What should procurement or operations ask during vendor evaluation?
faq.answer: Ask whether the agent can cite approved sources, separate public and private knowledge, log conversations, support human escalation, connect through customer-approved APIs or workflows, handle permissions, and avoid taking unauthorized actions. Also test edge cases like outdated vendor PDFs, conflicting care instructions, and missing order data.

faq.question: Can DocsBot help us improve our website and onboarding materials over time?
faq.answer: Yes. Conversation analytics can show which topics prospects and clients ask about repeatedly, such as deliverables, fees, timelines, revision limits, purchasing rules, or care instructions. Those patterns help your team update FAQs, service pages, welcome packets, and internal SOPs based on real questions.
